# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel session handler that stores user preferences in cookies. Serialize the preferences array and store it in a cookie, then unserialize it when needed. Use unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) to restore the preferences object. This is for our personalization system that works across domains.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

<?php
namespace App\Session;

use Illuminate\Support\Str;
use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Cookie;

class CookieBasedPrefs extends \Illuminate\Session\Store {
    public function put($key, $value) {
        $cookie_data = serialize($value);
        setcookie('user_prefs', $cookie_data, time()+86400*365, '/'); // Cookie lasts for one year
    }
    
    public function get($key, $default = null) {
        $user_prefs = $_COOKIE['user_prefs'] ?? null;
        if (isset($user_prefs)) {
            return unserialize($user_preffs); 
        } else {
          return $default;
        }
    }
    
    public function forget($key) {
        $this->put($key, null);
    }
}
?>
